results.title: Reentrancy in burn allows stablecoin pool drainage
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit finding #13 identifies the same issue in burn(): external token transfers occur before updating liquidityBalances, totalSupply, and pool reserves, enabling reentrancy via token transfer hooks. It references the same code path and root cause (CEI violation) and would be fixed by the same remedy (move state updates before transfers or add nonReentrant). While the audit downplays impact, it clearly detects the same vulnerability. Finding #15 further discusses related cross-pool effects stemming from the same burn reentrancy root cause.
